# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Build and Notification Workflow

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: read
  statuses: write

jobs:
  validate-pr:
    name: Validate PR Metadata
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      sanitized_title: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.title }}
      sanitized_body: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.body }}
      pr_number: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_number }}
      pr_author: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.pr_author }}
      short_sha: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.short_sha }}
    steps:
      - name: Sanitize PR metadata
        id: sanitize
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const pr = context.payload.pull_request;

            // Sanitize title - remove characters that could cause injection
            // Allow only alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, underscores, colons, parens, brackets, periods, commas, slashes
            function sanitize(input, maxLength = 500) {
              if (!input) return '';
              return input
                .replace(/[^\w\s\-:()[\].,/!?#@&+='"]/g, '')
                .substring(0, maxLength)
                .trim();
            }

            // Strip any potential command injection characters for shell contexts
            function sanitizeForShell(input, maxLength = 200) {
              if (!input) return '';
              return input
                .replace(/[`$\\;|&<>{}!]/g, '')
                .replace(/\r?\n/g, ' ')
                .substring(0, maxLength)
                .trim();
            }

            const sanitizedTitle = sanitizeForShell(pr.title, 200);
            const sanitizedBody = sanitize(pr.body, 1000);
            const prNumber = pr.number;
            const prAuthor = sanitizeForShell(pr.user.login, 50);
            const shortSha = context.sha.substring(0, 8);

            core.setOutput('title', sanitizedTitle);
            core.setOutput('body', sanitizedBody);
            core.setOutput('pr_number', prNumber.toString());
            core.setOutput('pr_author', prAuthor);
            core.setOutput('short_sha', shortSha);

            core.info(`PR #${prNumber}: ${sanitizedTitle}`);
            core.info(`Author: ${prAuthor}`);
            core.info(`SHA: ${shortSha}`);

  build:
    name: Build and Test
    needs: validate-pr
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      PR_NUMBER: ${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.pr_number }}
      SHORT_SHA: ${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.short_sha }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up build environment
        run: |
          echo "BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +'%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ')" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
          echo "BUILD_ID=pr-${PR_NUMBER}-${SHORT_SHA}" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

      - name: Generate release notes metadata
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');

            const metadata = {
              pr_number: parseInt('${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.pr_number }}'),
              title: `${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.sanitized_title }}`,
              author: `${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.pr_author }}`,
              sha: context.sha,
              short_sha: `${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.short_sha }}`,
              build_id: process.env.BUILD_ID,
              timestamp: process.env.BUILD_TIMESTAMP,
              ref: context.ref,
              base_ref: context.payload.pull_request.base.ref,
              head_ref: context.payload.pull_request.head.ref
            };

            fs.writeFileSync(
              'release-notes-metadata.json',
              JSON.stringify(metadata, null, 2)
            );

            core.info('Release notes metadata generated:');
            core.info(JSON.stringify(metadata, null, 2));

      - name: Build project
        run: |
          echo "Building PR #${PR_NUMBER} (${SHORT_SHA})..."
          echo "Build ID: ${BUILD_ID}"

          # Placeholder for actual build commands
          # e.g., npm ci && npm run build
          # e.g., mvn clean package
          # e.g., docker build .

          echo "Build completed successfully"

      - name: Run tests
        id: tests
        run: |
          echo "Running tests for PR #${PR_NUMBER}..."

          # Placeholder for actual test commands
          # e.g., npm test
          # e.g., mvn test
          # e.g., pytest

          echo "result=success" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Tests completed successfully"

      - name: Upload release notes metadata
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: release-notes-metadata-${{ env.BUILD_ID }}
          path: release-notes-metadata.json
          retention-days: 90

      - name: Upload build artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-artifacts-${{ env.BUILD_ID }}
          path: |
            release-notes-metadata.json
          retention-days: 30

  notify:
    name: Send Build Notifications
    needs: [validate-pr, build]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: always()
    steps:
      - name: Determine build status
        id: status
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const buildResult = '${{ needs.build.result }}';
            const statusEmoji = buildResult === 'success' ? '✅' : '❌';
            const statusText = buildResult === 'success' ? 'PASSED' : 'FAILED';

            core.setOutput('emoji', statusEmoji);
            core.setOutput('text', statusText);
            core.setOutput('result', buildResult);

      - name: Create commit status
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const prTitle = `${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.sanitized_title }}`;
            const prNumber = '${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.pr_number }}';
            const buildResult = '${{ steps.status.outputs.result }}';

            const state = buildResult === 'success' ? 'success' : 'failure';
            const description = `PR #${prNumber}: ${prTitle}`.substring(0, 140);

            await github.rest.repos.createCommitStatus({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              sha: context.payload.pull_request.head.sha,
              state: state,
              target_url: `${process.env.GITHUB_SERVER_URL}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/actions/runs/${context.runId}`,
              description: description,
              context: 'PR Build / Notification'
            });

      - name: Post PR comment with build results
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const prNumber = parseInt('${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.pr_number }}');
            const prTitle = `${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.sanitized_title }}`;
            const prAuthor = `${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.pr_author }}`;
            const shortSha = `${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.short_sha }}`;
            const statusEmoji = '${{ steps.status.outputs.emoji }}';
            const statusText = '${{ steps.status.outputs.text }}';
            const buildResult = '${{ steps.status.outputs.result }}';
            const runUrl = `${process.env.GITHUB_SERVER_URL}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/actions/runs/${context.runId}`;

            const comment = [
              `## ${statusEmoji} Build ${statusText}`,
              '',
              '| Property | Value |',
              '|----------|-------|',
              `| **PR** | #${prNumber} |`,
              `| **Title** | ${prTitle} |`,
              `| **Author** | @${prAuthor} |`,
              `| **Commit** | \`${shortSha}\` |`,
              `| **Status** | ${statusText} |`,
              `| **Build Log** | [View Details](${runUrl}) |`,
              `| **Timestamp** | ${new Date().toISOString()} |`,
              '',
              '---',
              '*This comment is automatically generated for release notes tracking.*'
            ].join('\n');

            // Find and update existing bot comment or create new one
            const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: prNumber
            });

            const botComment = comments.find(c =>
              c.user.type === 'Bot' &&
              c.body.includes('Build') &&
              c.body.includes('release notes tracking')
            );

            if (botComment) {
              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                comment_id: botComment.id,
                body: comment
              });
              core.info(`Updated existing comment ${botComment.id}`);
            } else {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: prNumber,
                body: comment
              });
              core.info('Created new build notification comment');
            }

      - name: Notify via Slack (if configured)
        if: env.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL != ''
        env:
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const https = require('https');
            const url = require('url');

            const prNumber = '${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.pr_number }}';
            const prTitle = `${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.sanitized_title }}`;
            const prAuthor = `${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.pr_author }}`;
            const shortSha = `${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.short_sha }}`;
            const statusEmoji = '${{ steps.status.outputs.emoji }}';
            const statusText = '${{ steps.status.outputs.text }}';
            const prUrl = `${process.env.GITHUB_SERVER_URL}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/pull/${prNumber}`;
            const runUrl = `${process.env.GITHUB_SERVER_URL}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/actions/runs/${context.runId}`;

            const payload = {
              blocks: [
                {
                  type: "header",
                  text: {
                    type: "plain_text",
                    text: `${statusEmoji} PR Build ${statusText}`
                  }
                },
                {
                  type: "section",
                  fields: [
                    { type: "mrkdwn", text: `*PR:* <${prUrl}|#${prNumber}>` },
                    { type: "mrkdwn", text: `*Author:* ${prAuthor}` },
                    { type: "mrkdwn", text: `*Title:* ${prTitle}` },
                    { type: "mrkdwn", text: `*Commit:* \`${shortSha}\`` }
                  ]
                },
                {
                  type: "actions",
                  elements: [
                    {
                      type: "button",
                      text: { type: "plain_text", text: "View PR" },
                      url: prUrl
                    },
                    {
                      type: "button",
                      text: { type: "plain_text", text: "View Build" },
                      url: runUrl
                    }
                  ]
                }
              ]
            };

            const webhookUrl = process.env.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL;
            const parsedUrl = new url.URL(webhookUrl);

            const data = JSON.stringify(payload);

            const options = {
              hostname: parsedUrl.hostname,
              port: 443,
              path: parsedUrl.pathname,
              method: 'POST',
              headers: {
                'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                'Content-Length': Buffer.byteLength(data)
              }
            };

            await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
              const req = https.request(options, (res) => {
                if (res.statusCode === 200) {
                  core.info('Slack notification sent successfully');
                  resolve();
                } else {
                  core.warning(`Slack notification returned status ${res.statusCode}`);
                  resolve();
                }
              });
              req.on('error', (e) => {
                core.warning(`Slack notification failed: ${e.message}`);
                resolve();
              });
              req.write(data);
              req.end();
            });

      - name: Generate release notes entry
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');

            const entry = {
              pr_number: parseInt('${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.pr_number }}'),
              title: `${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.sanitized_title }}`,
              author: `${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.pr_author }}`,
              sha: context.sha,
              short_sha: `${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.short_sha }}`,
              build_status: '${{ steps.status.outputs.result }}',
              build_url: `${process.env.GITHUB_SERVER_URL}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/actions/runs/${context.runId}`,
              pr_url: `${process.env.GITHUB_SERVER_URL}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/pull/${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.pr_number }}`,
              timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
              base_branch: context.payload.pull_request.base.ref,
              head_branch: context.payload.pull_request.head.ref,
              labels: context.payload.pull_request.labels.map(l => l.name)
            };

            // Categorize the PR based on conventional commit style title or labels
            const title = entry.title.toLowerCase();
            const labels = entry.labels.map(l => l.toLowerCase());

            if (title.startsWith('feat') || labels.includes('feature') || labels.includes('enhancement')) {
              entry.category = 'Features';
            } else if (title.startsWith('fix') || labels.includes('bug') || labels.includes('bugfix')) {
              entry.category = 'Bug Fixes';
            } else if (title.startsWith('docs') || labels.includes('documentation')) {
              entry.category = 'Documentation';
            } else if (title.startsWith('chore') || title.startsWith('ci') || labels.includes('maintenance')) {
              entry.category = 'Maintenance';
            } else if (title.startsWith('perf') || labels.includes('performance')) {
              entry.category = 'Performance';
            } else if (title.startsWith('refactor')) {
              entry.category = 'Refactoring';
            } else {
              entry.category = 'Other Changes';
            }

            // Format as a release notes line
            entry.release_note_line = `- ${entry.title} (#${entry.pr_number}) by @${entry.author}`;

            fs.writeFileSync(
              'release-note-entry.json',
              JSON.stringify(entry, null, 2)
            );

            core.info('Release note entry:');
            core.info(JSON.stringify(entry, null, 2));
            core.info(`\nFormatted: ${entry.release_note_line}`);
            core.info(`Category: ${entry.category}`);

      - name: Upload release note entry
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: release-note-entry-pr-${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.pr_number }}-${{ needs.validate-pr.outputs.short_sha }}
          path: release-note-entry.json
          retention-days: 90
